intro: Willoughbya trinervis, commonly known as Willoughbya, is a plant species native to Australia. Traditionally used in Aboriginal medicine, the plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating wounds and infections. The bioactive constituents of Willoughbya trinervis include phenolic compounds, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include a tea infusion and a topical gel or ointment, which are used to promote wound healing and soothe skin irritations.
